body_txt: A character who fits Jewish stereotypes, possibly played by an actor who is Jewish in real life, but who is never actually stated to be a Jew on the show. In some cases, the show might present contradictory evidence on both sides. Evidence in support can include a wide variety of broad Jewish stereotypes, whether positive or negative, including being a Brainy Brunette , a Bookworm , a smartass , a cheapskate , or a greedy businessman or banker . They might be prone to Self-Deprecation , arguing or complaining . They may also display behavior that simply implies a Jewish background, such as showing a particular hatred for antisemitic groups. All of this may or may not be combined with traits that are not stereotypical of Jews, such as being blond or Asian. Their behavior might also imply a different background, such as wearing Christmas attire. Note that having Yiddish as a Second Language is a clue but is not conclusive. Also, most stereotypically "Jewish" names are not specifically Jewish and so are supporting evidence but not conclusive proof. If the character lives in New York City or some equivalent , you can usually expect at least some of this. About 12% of New Yorkers are Ashkenazi Jewish, and they've had a great deal of impact on the culture. As Lenny Bruce said, all New Yorkers are at least a little Jewish. This is the exact opposite of Informed Judaism , which is when a Jewish character's faith is plainly stated, usually as an afterthought, but never really exhibited by their thoughts or actions and has no relevance in the plot. If a character is non-human and displays stereotypical Jewish behavior, it's Space Jews . noreallife
